title: Herd the logs
prompt: The * wildcard means 'any characters', so one command can act on many files: *.log matches every name ending in .log. The lab root is littered with files. Move EVERY .log file into logs/ in a single command — and leave data.txt and readme.md exactly where they are.

details.restate: All four .log files end up inside logs/; the root keeps data.txt and readme.md and has no .log files left. The point is doing it with ONE command, not four.

io.because: * expands to every matching filename before the command even runs

terms.meaning: a pattern piece meaning 'any characters' — the shell replaces it with every matching filename

help.plain: mv accepts many files at once, and the wildcard hands it every .log in one go.
help.analogy: A magnet over a parts bin — it lifts every screw at once and leaves the plastic bits behind.
help.mnemonic: mv *.log logs/ — the pattern does the picking.
help.nudge: mv can take a whole list of files with the destination folder last. What pattern produces 'every file ending in .log'?
